questions.q: Bimbisara was a king of which ancient kingdom?
questions.explanation: Bimbisara ruled the kingdom of Magadha in the Ganges plain.

questions.q: Bimbisara belonged to which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Bimbisara was a ruler of the Haryanka dynasty of Magadha.

questions.q: In roughly which century BCE did Bimbisara rule?
questions.explanation: Bimbisara ruled Magadha in the 6th century BCE.

questions.q: Bimbisara was a contemporary of which two famous religious teachers?
questions.explanation: Bimbisara lived at the same time as the Buddha and Mahavira and patronised both.

questions.q: Which neighbouring kingdom did Bimbisara annex into Magadha?
questions.explanation: Bimbisara annexed the kingdom of Anga, extending Magadha eastward.

questions.q: Bimbisara is often regarded as the real founder of which power?
questions.explanation: Bimbisara is seen as the founder of Magadha's rise to imperial greatness.

questions.q: What was a key method Bimbisara used to expand Magadha's influence peacefully?
questions.explanation: Bimbisara strengthened Magadha through marriage alliances with neighbouring royal houses.

questions.q: Bimbisara's capital was the city of what?
questions.explanation: Bimbisara ruled from Rajagriha (Rajgir), the early capital of Magadha.

questions.q: Bimbisara was an early patron of which two traditions?
questions.explanation: Bimbisara patronised both Buddhism and Jainism, the two great heterodox movements of his age.

questions.q: Who succeeded Bimbisara as king of Magadha?
questions.explanation: Bimbisara was succeeded by his son Ajatashatru.

questions.q: According to Buddhist tradition, how did Bimbisara meet his end?
options: He was imprisoned and died under his son Ajatashatru
He died in battle against Kosala
He abdicated and became a monk
questions.explanation: Buddhist tradition holds that Ajatashatru imprisoned Bimbisara, who died in captivity.

questions.q: Bimbisara's marriage alliance with Kosala brought which territory or benefit to Magadha?
options: A village in Kashi as dowry
questions.explanation: His marriage to a Kosalan princess brought a Kashi village as dowry, linking Magadha to Kosala.

questions.q: The annexation of Anga gave Magadha access to which strategic asset?
options: The pearl fisheries of the south
Eastern trade and river routes to the sea
questions.explanation: Anga's ports opened eastern trade routes down the Ganges toward the sea for Magadha.

questions.q: Which Kosalan princess was a wife of Bimbisara, cementing a marriage alliance?
questions.explanation: Kosaladevi, sister of King Prasenajit of Kosala, was married to Bimbisara.

questions.q: Bimbisara reportedly sent his royal physician Jivaka to attend to whom?
questions.explanation: Bimbisara's renowned physician Jivaka is said to have tended the Buddha.

questions.q: Bimbisara's marriage to a Lichchhavi princess connected Magadha to which confederacy?
questions.explanation: His marriage to a Lichchhavi princess linked Magadha to the Vajji confederacy centred on Vaishali.

questions.q: Which long-reigning king of Kosala was both Bimbisara's contemporary and brother-in-law?
questions.explanation: Prasenajit of Kosala was Bimbisara's contemporary and, through marriage, his brother-in-law.

questions.q: Bimbisara is credited with developing an early efficient system of what?
questions.explanation: Bimbisara is remembered for organising Magadha's administration with appointed officials.

questions.q: Which contemporary king of Avanti was a rival of Bimbisara's Magadha?
questions.explanation: Pradyota, the powerful king of Avanti, was a noted rival of Bimbisara.

questions.q: Bimbisara's policy of marriage alliances primarily served what aim?
options: Building a network of coastal forts
Securing borders and expanding Magadhan influence
questions.explanation: His alliances secured Magadha's frontiers and extended its diplomatic and territorial reach.

questions.q: Roughly how long is Bimbisara traditionally said to have reigned?
questions.explanation: Tradition credits Bimbisara with a long reign of roughly half a century.

questions.q: Which queen of Bimbisara was a Lichchhavi princess and, by some accounts, the mother of Ajatashatru?
questions.explanation: Chellana, a Lichchhavi princess, is named in tradition as a queen of Bimbisara and mother of Ajatashatru.

questions.q: Jain tradition refers to Bimbisara by which alternative name?
questions.explanation: In Jain texts Bimbisara is known as Shrenika, a devotee linked to Mahavira.

questions.q: Bimbisara is said to have gifted a pleasure garden near Rajagriha to the Buddhist sangha. What was it called?
questions.explanation: Bimbisara donated the Venuvana, or Bamboo Grove, at Rajagriha to the Buddhist community.

questions.q: The Lichchhavi princess whom Bimbisara married was the daughter of which Vaishali chief?
questions.explanation: Chetaka, the leading Lichchhavi chief of Vaishali, was father of the princess wed to Bimbisara.

questions.q: Which third major marriage alliance did Bimbisara form, reaching into the northwest of his realm?
options: A Pandya princess from the far south
A Madra princess from the Punjab region
questions.explanation: Tradition records Bimbisara marrying a Madra princess, extending alliances toward the northwest.

questions.q: Bimbisara's defeated rival in Anga is traditionally named as which king?
questions.explanation: Bimbisara is said to have defeated Brahmadatta of Anga to annex that kingdom.

questions.q: Bimbisara's reign is traditionally dated by many historians to roughly which span?
questions.explanation: Many traditional chronologies place Bimbisara's reign around 544 to 492 BCE, though dates are debated.

questions.q: Bimbisara's hill capital Rajagriha is associated with which famous nearby peak linked to the Buddha?
questions.explanation: Gridhrakuta, or Vulture Peak, near Rajagriha was a site where the Buddha is said to have taught.

questions.q: Bimbisara's expansionist policies set a precedent later carried much further by which dynasty from Magadha?
questions.explanation: Bimbisara's foundations for Magadhan power were ultimately built upon by the Mauryan dynasty.
